executor_handoff.system_instructions: Preserve existing patterns. Follow scope exactly. Do NOT widen scope. Use ONLY the chosen path below. Readonly elsewhere. Swift 6 strict concurrency. macOS14+. Use serena for edits, xcode-tools for build/test. Project conventions (styleguide.md / unit-testing.md) override any skill defaults.

task_brief.objective: Redesign report.md (markdown report only — NOT report.json). Three goals: (1) Delete "Findings" section and "Session Details" section entirely. (2) Replace flat per-group "Playlist Information" dump with one self-contained block PER playlist: heading with health glyph + verdict, flat property bullets, merged "Timing" subsection, and a per-playlist "Findings" subsection holding that playlist's findings. (3) Fix the "evidence: evidence:" double-print bug. Report must let a NON-engineer see at a glance whether each stream/playlist is fine.
task_brief.why: Current report triple-books findings (Findings + Incident Timeline + Session Details), repeats identical findings verbatim, and renders every playlist's groups at flat ### with no parent header — unreadable. Per-playlist consolidation + a green/yellow/red glyph gives instant triage.
task_brief.repo_signals: - REWRITE TARGET: ValistreamCore/Sources/ValistreamCore/Session/SessionReportBuilder.swift - buildMarkdown @193-381. Keep: identity table @ ~205-218, Summary @ ~220-237, Legend @ ~342-360. DELETE: Findings block @ ~262-320, Session Details block @ ~362-380, old Playlist Information loop @ ~326-340. TWEAK: Incident Timeline loop @ ~240-256. - markdownEvidence @387-404 — `.single` returns "evidence: `\(path)`"; `.pair` returns "evidence: " + parts. THIS is the double-print (caller already prints "- Evidence: "). - PlaylistInfo (nested) @15-47: id, kind(PlaylistKind), role(PlaylistRole?), url, selected, excludedByChoice, refreshCount, stalenessEpisodes. - buildReport @406-480 = JSON path. DO NOT TOUCH (keeps full findings incl. encryption-info). - FORMATTER: ValistreamCore/Sources/ValistreamCore/Output/PlaylistInfoFormatter.swift - groups(for:) @36-43, masterGroups @45-69, mediaGroups @71-101. Private helpers: field(), yesNo, seconds, range, listed, declared, segmentDuration, renditionCounts. - Group titles today — master:[Identity,Structure,Variant declarations,Protection]; media:[Identity,Timing,Sequence,Features,Protection]. - PlaylistInfoFieldGroup @22-31 (title, fields). PlaylistInfoField (label, value). - groups(for:) is REPORT-ONLY (sole non-test caller = buildMarkdown). Safe to delete after rewrite. - DATA: ValistreamCore/Sources/ValistreamCore/Playlist/PlaylistInformation.swift:10-144 (playlistID, kind .master/.media, master: MasterInfo?, media: MediaInfo?; MasterInfo/MediaInfo same file). info.media.playlistType holds "LIVE"/"VOD"/etc. - Finding: ValistreamCore/Sources/ValistreamCore/Validation/Finding.swift:14-156. id@89, ruleId@90, severity@92 (.error/.warning/.info @25-29), resource(URL)@94, observedAt@97, message@98. Encryption-info finding has ruleId == "TOOL.encryption", severity .info. - PlaylistKind: Playlist/PlaylistModel.swift:40-43 (master/media). - PlaylistRole: Session/PlaylistLoader.swift:11-16 (variant/audio/subtitles/iframe). Master = nil role. - IncidentTimeline: Session/IncidentTimeline.swift:22-136. Entry(sequence,kind,findingAnchor "finding-<id>",summary). Finding summary @ ~83 = "<Sev> finding in <resource.lastPathComponent>" (the ugly "...m3u8"). R11 comment @ ~36-37: timeline entries are LINK-ONLY, no message. Model already drops .info findings — encryption-info never reaches timeline. - CALLER: Session/ValidationSession+Reporting.swift — buildMarkdown call @141-153 passes playlistInfos, recordedFindings, aliasRegistry, evidenceByFindingID, incidentTimeline, orderedInformation. orderedInformation sort @ ~115-122 (master-first then playlistID asc) becomes DEAD (new layout orders off role inside builder). Leave caller untouched. - Aliases: aliasRegistry.alias(for: url)?.alias ; aliasRegistry.all (entry.alias/.url/.role/.attributes). - Reuse as-is: EvidenceResolver, EvidenceReference, ReportTimestampFormatter.format(_:timeZone:).
task_brief.scope: IN: - SessionReportBuilder.buildMarkdown: delete Findings + Session Details blocks; replace Playlist Information loop with per-playlist blocks; alias-swap timeline finding rows; source Summary + verdict from encryption-info-filtered findings. - SessionReportBuilder.markdownEvidence: drop leading "evidence: " in .single and .pair. - PlaylistInfoFormatter: add reportHeaderFields(for:) + reportTimingFields(for:); delete groups() + masterGroups/mediaGroups (report-only) once nothing references them. - Add private Verdict enum + PlaylistBlock struct + role-ordering helper in builder. - Update tests (see validation). OUT (do NOT touch): - report.json / buildReport / Report payload / SummaryPayload. JSON keeps ALL findings. - IncidentTimeline model + R11 (timeline stays message-free; only the RENDER swaps filename->alias). - Rule engine / finding emission (encryption-info dropped at md-render only). - Caller ValidationSession+Reporting.swift (its orderedInformation sort left dead, harmless). - Identity table, Summary layout, Legend table.
task_brief.implementation_path:
A. markdownEvidence — remove its own prefix: case .single(let path): return "`\(path)`" case .pair(...): return parts.joined(separator: ", ") // drop leading "evidence: " (.unavailable unchanged.) Result line: "- Evidence: `path`".
B. Filter once at top of buildMarkdown, after computing counts inputs: let shown = findings.filter { $0.ruleId != "TOOL.encryption" } Use `shown` for: Summary counts (errors/warnings/infos @ ~221-223), per-playlist findings, verdict. (Timeline already excludes .info — unaffected.)
C. Incident Timeline loop — for entries with findingAnchor, render glyph + ALIAS + link, NO message: let fid = anchor without "finding-" prefix let who = findingsByID[fid].flatMap { aliasRegistry.alias(for: $0.resource)?.alias } ?? entry.summary md += "- \(prefix) \(who) — [Finding \(fid)](#\(anchor))\n" Build findingsByID = Dictionary(findings.map { ($0.id, $0) }, uniquingKeysWith: { a,_ in a }). Non-finding (lifecycle/operational) rows unchanged. R11 intact (no message text).
D. Replace old Playlist Information loop with per-playlist blocks: let infoByID = Dictionary(playlistInformation.map { ($0.playlistID, $0) }, uniquingKeysWith: { a,_ in a }) let fByURL = Dictionary(grouping: shown, by: \.resource) let ordered = playlists.enumerated().sorted { (roleRank($0.element), $0.offset) < (roleRank($1.element), $1.offset) // stable }.map(\.element) where roleRank(p) = p.kind == .master ? 0 : switch p.role { .variant:1; .audio:2; .subtitles:3; .iframe:4; nil:5 } For each p in ordered: render block(info: p, information: infoByID[p.id], findings: (fByURL[p.url] ?? []).sorted { $0.id < $1.id }). NOTE: NO "## Playlists" wrapper — each block is top-level ##.
E. Block render (see exact spec in code_level_guidance): - Verdict from block findings: any .error -> .problems; else any .warning -> .needsAttention; else .healthy. - Heading: "## \(verdict.glyph) \(displayID)" + (isMaster ? " · Master manifest" : ""). displayID = aliasRegistry.alias(for: p.url)?.alias ?? p.id ; isMaster = p.kind == .master. - Italic subtitle (see spec). - Flat bullets from PlaylistInfoFormatter.reportHeaderFields(for: information) as "- \(label): \(value)". - If reportTimingFields(for: information) non-nil (media): "### Timing" + its bullets. - If block findings non-empty: "### Findings" + one "#### \(sevGlyph) Finding \(f.id)" subsection each.
F. PlaylistInfoFormatter additions (reuse existing private field()/yesNo()/seconds()/range()/...): reportHeaderFields(for:) — DROP "Playlist ID" field. master: Type(Master), HLS version, Independent segments, Variants, Media playlists, Renditions, I-frame streams, Resolutions, Codecs, Bandwidth, Frame rate, Session protection. media: Type, HLS version, End list, Independent segments, I-frames only, Segment format, Byte ranges, Program date time, Protection. reportTimingFields(for:) -> [PlaylistInfoField]? : master -> nil. media -> Segments, Total duration, Target duration, Segment duration, Media sequence, Discontinuity sequence, Discontinuities. (old Timing + Sequence merged) Then delete groups()/masterGroups()/mediaGroups().
task_brief.code_level_guidance:
VERDICT mapping (private enum in builder): .healthy -> glyph "✅", word "Healthy" .needsAttention -> glyph "⚠️", word "Needs attention" .problems -> glyph "🔴", word "Problems" FINDING severity glyph (for #### header + timeline prefix already exists): .error 🔴, .warning 🟡, .info 🔵.
SUBTITLE (italic, parts joined " · "), exact order: part1 = verdict.word part2 = isMaster ? "\(variantCount) variants" : information.media.playlistType // e.g. "LIVE" part3 = "\(refreshCount) refresh" + (refreshCount == 1 ? "" : "es") if warnings > 0: "\(warnings) warning" + (warnings==1 ? "" : "s") if errors > 0: "\(errors) error" + (errors==1 ? "" : "s") if !selected: (excludedByChoice ? "excluded" : "not selected") Wrap whole line in *...*. variantCount = information.master.variantCount (master only).
FINDING subsection body (per finding, severity is .warning/.error after filter): "#### \(sevGlyph) Finding \(f.id)\n\n" "- Severity: \(f.severity.rawValue.capitalized)\n" "- Rule: `\(f.ruleId)`\n" "- Message: \(f.message)\n" "- Observed: \(ReportTimestampFormatter.format(f.observedAt, timeZone: timeZone))\n" "- Evidence: \(markdownEvidence(evidenceByFindingID[f.id] ?? resolver.resolve(f, aliases: aliasRegistry, artifactIndex: artifactIndex, fallbackID: displayID)))\n\n" (Reuse the EvidenceResolver()/resolve(...) pattern from the deleted Findings block.)
ANCHOR RISK (must verify): timeline links target "#finding-<id>". GitHub header-slug lowercases, strips emoji + punctuation, spaces->'-', strips leading/trailing '-'. So "#### 🟡 Finding f1" SHOULD slug to "finding-f1". After build, OPEN the generated report.md and CONFIRM a timeline link resolves to its #### subsection. If slug differs, FALLBACK: header "#### Finding \(f.id)" (no glyph) and keep severity in the "- Severity:" line. Do not ship broken anchors.
ORDERING: Swift sort is NOT stable — the (roleRank, originalOffset) tuple key guarantees determinism. Within .variant, input order already res-desc (V0..V4 = 1080..360); do NOT add resolution parsing.
TARGET RENDER (authoritative oracle — real session 20260616-111043-6aa): ## Summary **Completed with warnings.** 0 errors, 6 warnings, 0 informational findings. - Playlists: 8 - Refreshes: 1
## Incident Timeline - 🟡 master — [Finding f1](#finding-f1) - 🟡 master — [Finding f2](#finding-f2) ... f3 f4 f5 f6 ...
## ⚠️ master · Master manifest *Needs attention · 5 variants · 1 refresh · 6 warnings* - Type: Master - HLS version: 7 - Independent segments: No - Variants: 5 - Media playlists: 5 - Renditions: audio 1, subtitles 1 - I-frame streams: 0 - Resolutions: 640x360, 960x540, 1280x720, 1920x1080 - Codecs: avc1.64001f, avc1.640020, avc1.64002a, mp4a.40.2 - Bandwidth: 1387000-9297000 - Frame rate: 50.0 - Session protection: DRM (com.apple.streamingkeydelivery) ### Findings #### 🟡 Finding f1 - Severity: Warning - Rule: `APPLE.average-bandwidth` - Message: EXT-X-STREAM-INF should declare AVERAGE-BANDWIDTH. - Observed: 2026-06-16T11:10:45.925+02:00 - Evidence: `playlists/master/master_0.m3u8` #### 🟡 Finding f2 ... (f2-f5 identical; f6 = APPLE.independent-segments)
## ✅ 1080p_avc1-mp4a *Healthy · LIVE · 1 refresh* - Type: LIVE - HLS version: 7 - End list: No - Independent segments: No - I-frames only: No - Segment format: m4v - Byte ranges: No - Program date time: Yes - Protection: DRM (com.apple.streamingkeydelivery) ### Timing - Segments: 6 - Total duration: 24 s - Target duration: 4 s - Segment duration: median 4 s, range 4-4 s - Media sequence: 445400253 - Discontinuity sequence: 0 - Discontinuities: 0 (NO ### Findings — encryption-info dropped, no other findings.)
... then 1080p_avc1-mp4a_2, 720p, 540p, 360p (variant, res-desc), audio_nor, subs_nor. subs_nor: Protection: None ; ✅ ; no Findings. ## Legend (unchanged table)
task_brief.entry_points: ValistreamCore/Sources/ValistreamCore/Session/SessionReportBuilder.swift (buildMarkdown, markdownEvidence) ValistreamCore/Sources/ValistreamCore/Output/PlaylistInfoFormatter.swift (add report fields, delete groups) ValistreamCore/Sources/ValistreamCore/Playlist/PlaylistInformation.swift (read MasterInfo/MediaInfo fields) ValistreamCore/Sources/ValistreamCore/Session/IncidentTimeline.swift (read only — R11)
task_brief.search_fallback: grep "## Findings" / "Session Details" / "Playlist Information" in SessionReportBuilder for the blocks. grep "func markdownEvidence" for the bug. grep "PlaylistInfoFormatter.groups" for all consumers. grep ".ruleId == " / "TOOL.encryption" for the encryption rule id. grep "func buildMarkdown(" for tests.
task_brief.acceptance_criteria: - report.md has NO "## Findings" and NO "## Session Details" sections. - Each playlist renders ONE "## <glyph> <id>" block; master adds " · Master manifest". - Glyph/verdict: 0 warn/err -> ✅ Healthy; >=1 warning -> ⚠️ Needs attention; >=1 error -> 🔴 Problems. - Italic subtitle matches spec (verdict · kind|N variants · N refresh[es] · [warnings] · [errors] · [status]). - Media block: flat bullets (NO "Playlist ID") then "### Timing" (Timing+Sequence merged). Master block: flat bullets only (Identity-ID + Structure + Variant decl + Protection), NO Timing. - Playlists ordered master -> variant(res-desc input order) -> audio -> subtitles -> iframe. - Per-playlist "### Findings" present ONLY when that playlist has >=1 non-encryption finding; each finding a "#### <glyph> Finding <id>" subsection with Severity/Rule/Message/Observed/Evidence. No dedupe. - Encryption-info (TOOL.encryption) findings absent from md body AND Summary counts. Still present in report.json. - "evidence: evidence:" GONE — evidence renders "- Evidence: `path`". - Incident Timeline finding rows show alias (not "...m3u8") + "[Finding fN](#finding-fN)"; links RESOLVE to the relocated subsections (verify in generated file). - report.json byte-identical to before this change.
task_brief.validation: - Build scheme `Valistream` (xcode-tools BuildProject) -> 0 errors. - Run `Valistream.xctestplan` (RunAllTests) -> all pass. Update expectations in: ValistreamCoreTests/Session/ReportMarkdownTests.swift, ValistreamCoreTests/Session/SessionReportTests.swift, ValistreamCoreTests/Session/SessionReportTimelineInfoTests.swift (@77 asserts each group.title — rewrite to new headers), Valistream/ValistreamIntegrationTests/IncidentTimelineReportTests.swift (timeline alias text), Valistream/ValistreamIntegrationTests/EvidenceInOutputTests.swift (@71 — drop "evidence: evidence:"), Valistream/ValistreamIntegrationTests/PlaylistIDSchemeTests.swift (id-presence under new headers). - Add focused Swift Testing coverage (unit-testing.md conventions): verdict glyph mapping, subtitle format, master-vs-media block shape, encryption-info suppression, per-playlist finding routing, evidence single-prefix, timeline link anchor resolves.
